Action-oriented objective: Navigate the HRBA course safely, including the player controls, certificate rule, private reflections, and practical tool pathway.
Capacity area: Digital learning readiness and safe participation
CSO practice area: Course participation; reflective learning; safe use of digital tools
HRBA principle / concept: Safe learning, privacy, accessibility, adult learning
Learning block(s): M1-LMS-00; M1-PLAYER-00; M1-PLAYER-HELP; M1-S1-05; M1-S1-06; M1-S1-07
Portfolio / practical output: Safe learning agreement acknowledgement; optional portfolio setup
Measurement evidence: Agreement accepted; orientation check completed; no sensitive data entered

Action-oriented objective: Explain why HRBA matters for local CSO practice and why good intentions are not enough without rights-based questions.
CSO practice area: Project design; community engagement; accountability; donor-aligned quality
Assessment / check: Formative check in Module 1 quiz; scenario feedback
Portfolio / practical output: Optional private reflection on why HRBA matters for learner’s work
Measurement evidence: Learner completes scenario/relevance cards and views feedback

Action-oriented objective: Describe the shift from treating people as beneficiaries to recognizing people and groups as rights-holders with voice, agency, and claims.
CSO practice area: Problem analysis; programme framing; community participation
Assessment / check: Scenario decision: Ask Better Questions Before Acting
Portfolio / practical output: Selected personal/team shift theme in M1-S4-04 or portfolio checkpoint
Measurement evidence: Scenario completed; feedback viewed; shift selected

Action-oriented objective: Distinguish charity-based, needs-based, service-delivery, and rights-based practice framings using simple CSO examples.
CSO practice area: Project design; service delivery; advocacy positioning
Portfolio / practical output: Private selection of one shift for CSO practice
Measurement evidence: Sorting completed with feedback; retry allowed

Action-oriented objective: Identify rights-holders, duty-bearers, CSO support roles, and other stakeholders in a realistic local CSO scenario.
HRBA principle / concept: Rights-holder / duty-bearer relationship; CSO bridge role
Portfolio / practical output: Draft rights-holder/duty-bearer understanding; optional map resource
Measurement evidence: Matching completed; diagram explored; misconception cards viewed

Action-oriented objective: Complete a private HRBA starting-point self-assessment and select two priority areas for continued learning.
CSO practice area: Organizational learning; project quality improvement; capacity development
Measurement evidence: Survey submitted; result viewed; two priority domains selected

Action-oriented objective: Save a safe Module 1 portfolio checkpoint and prepare for Module 2: Foundations of HRBA.
CSO practice area: Portfolio building; action planning; continuous learning
Assessment / check: Module 1 formative quiz and feedback summary
Portfolio / practical output: Portfolio checkpoint; next action to Module 2
Measurement evidence: Quiz completed; feedback viewed; module completion and progress state saved
